mechanism_narrative: Persistently low and stable federal funds rate near the zero lower bound keeps the equity discount rate compressed, supporting continued multiple expansion and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.16 at t-1mo, near historic lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during low-rate periods are not significantly different from full-sample average (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades and risk-off equity flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In periods where the 0.3pp trigger fires, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth and housing starts both accelerate simultaneously, this joint strength in consumer and residential investment demand signals broadening economic momentum that is not captured by either series alone, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: The joint-trigger periods show forward 20d return distribution statistically indistinguishable from unconditional distribution (t-test p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which mechanically flow into near-term corporate revenue expectations and support short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Sign of monthly INDPRO change fails to predict direction of forward 5-day SPX returns with better than 50% hit rate over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, continued strong payroll growth signals tightening labor markets that raise wage-cost pressures and increase the probability of future Fed tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as markets price in higher discount rates.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=130515k at t-1mo indicating multi-year expansion
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with low policy rate
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS 3-month growth is followed by positive 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of late-cycle instances, contradicting the negative direction claim, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of rising consumer price inflation with a stagnant federal funds rate implies a falling real policy rate, which is accommodative for equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month; this effect is not present when examining either series independently.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth rising AND FEDFUNDS flat or falling
failure_condition: Estimated real rate proxy (CPIAUCSL YoY minus FEDFUNDS) trending down fails to correlate positively with forward 20d returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below a threshold level signals weakening residential investment and consumer confidence, foreshadowing broader demand contraction and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1000 thousand units on a 3-month average basis
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=596.0 at t-2mo, well below historical norm
failure_condition: Instances where HOUST is below the 1000k threshold show forward 60d SPX returns positive in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In an early-recovery regime, simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and payroll employment growth signals a self-reinforcing supply-side rebound that outpaces market expectations, generating short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns over the next week; neither series alone reliably captures this rebound signal.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM change > 0 AND PAYEMS MoM change > 150k
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods produce forward 5-day SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p>0.05), or hit rate below 52% over any rolling 2-year window.
